request.question: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?

title: Krystal Jung
Black Pearl (K-Pop Group)
Jewelry (band)
Unexpected Love (2017 film)
Kiss (Dara song)
Kim Chung-ha
D.Holic
Kwon So-hyun
GP Basic
Lina (South Korean singer)
sentences: Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.
She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.
The Ballad.
Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
Black Pearl (Hangul: 블랙펄) was a South Korean girl group official made up of Oh Nami, Hwl Eun, Young Joo, and Mikka.
When the group debuted in 2007 they marketed as the new girl group to dominate the girl group charts in the following years of 2007 and 2008 alongside some artist Wonder Girls, and Kara.
They were also known to follow the steps of SG Wannabe, SeeYa, and M2M.
In 2009 Mikka left due to personal reasons.
New member Jung Min was added by 2010 or 2011.
As for the 2010 and therefore the group only consisted of the following 4 until their disbandment in 2012.
Jewelry (Korean: 쥬얼리 ) was a South Korean girl group formed in 2001 by Star Empire Entertainment.
The group was the longest-running Korean girl group (with more than 2 members) until their disbandment in 2015, surpassing super-group Baby V.O.X.
Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!
Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.
"Kiss" (Korean: 입맞춤) is a dance single performed by South Korean idol singer Sandara Park, a visual and member of the girl group 2NE1.
The song was used as part of a promotional campaign for Cass Beer.
It features the vocals of her co-member, CL of South Korean girl group 2NE1.
The song was released as her first official single in South Korea and was later included in the album "To Anyone".
Kim Chung-ha (born Kim Chan-mi on February 9, 1996), better known by the mononym Chungha, is a South Korean singer.
She is best known for finishing fourth in Mnet's girl group survival show "Produce 101" and as a former member of the now disbanded South Korean girl group I.O.I.
D.Holic (Hangul: 디홀릭) was a South Korean girl group formed by Star Road Entertainment (formerly H.Mate Entertainment) in 2014 with five members.
Nine left the group in August 2015, due to personal reasons, and was replaced by new member, Hwajung.
In July 2016, it was revealed through teasers that Danbee and Duri had decided to leave the group, new member, EJ, was added to the line-up.
In February 2017, it was confirmed through a performance that members Hami and Hwajung had departed from the group.
They were temporary replaced with new members, Nayoung and Youjin, although they never officially made their debut.
In July 2017, EJ announced that she would be leaving the group to pursue a modelling career.
The group informally disbanded after the departure of all but one member, with the aim of re-debuting the remaining member, Rena, into a new girl group within a year.
The group has released one mini-album: "Chewy" (2015) and three single albums: "D.Holic Dark With Dignity" (2014), "Murphy & Sally" (2015), and "Color Me Rad" (2016).
Kwon So-hyun (born August 30, 1994), is a South Korean singer and actress.
She was known as a member of the South Korean girl group 4Minute, under Cube Entertainment.
She is also a former member of the South Korean girl group, Orange.
Before June 15, 2016 (end of her contract with Cube), Sohyun left 4Minute and the record label along with members Nam Ji-hyun, Heo Ga-yoon, and Jeon Ji-yoon.
GP Basic (Korean: 지피 베이직 ) was a South Korean girl group who debuted on August 15, 2010.
At the time of their debut, they were considered to be the "youngest" South Korean girl group with an average age of below 13.5.
The age of their youngest member, Janey, caused controversy followed by the debut of a much younger girl group, G-story, who were all below 10 years old.
This led to new broadcasting regulations in South Korea.
Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.
She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.
She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.
She currently active as a musical actress.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Which actress that was a member of South Korean girl group is co-staring in Unexpected Love?
hop1_docs: Ryu Hyo-young | Ryu Hyoyoung (born April 22, 1993), better known by the mononym Hyoyoung, is a South Korean actress, model, and rapper.  She was a member of the South Korean co-ed group Coed School and the girl group F-ve Dolls.
Lee Soomi | Lee Soomi (; born March 3, 1989) is a South Korean singer, dancer, rapper, and actress.  She is a former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls, and coed group Coed School.

Shoo (singer) | Yoo Soo-young or Shū Kunimitsu (born 23 October 1981), better known as Shoo, is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She is best known as a member of South Korean girl group S.E.S..  In 2010, she released her debut solo extended play, "Devote One's Love."

Gain (singer) | Son Ga-in (born September 20, 1987), better known mononymously as Gain, is a South Korean singer, actress and entertainer.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls and for her appearances alongside Jo Kwon from 2AM for TV shows "We Got Married" and "All My Love".  As a solo artist she has released six EPs.

Lina (South Korean singer) | Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984), better known by her stage name Lina is a South Korean singer and musical actress.  She debuted in 2002 as a member of South Korean R&B duo Isak N Jiyeon and disbanded in 2004.  She later re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace in 2005 and the group currently being inactive.  She currently active as a musical actress.

Yura (South Korean singer) | Kim Ah-young (born November 6, 1992), better known by her stage name Yura, is a South Korean singer and actress.  She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day.

Krystal Jung | Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994), better known by the mononym Krystal, is an American singer and actress based in South Korea.  She debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x) and has further participated in S.M. Entertainment's project group S.M.  The Ballad.  Aside from group activities, she has also participated in various television dramas such as "" (2011–12), "The Heirs" (2013), "My Lovely Girl" (2014), and "The Bride of Habaek" (2017).
response.summary_1:
Candidates (from passages): 
- Ryu Hyoyoung — "Ryu Hyoyoung (born April 22, 1993) ... She was a member of the South Korean co-ed group Coed School and the girl group F-ve Dolls." 
- Lee Soomi — "Lee Soomi (born March 3, 1989) ... She is a former member of girl groups SeeYa, F-ve Dolls, and coed group Coed School." 
- Shoo — "Yoo Soo-young ... (born October 23, 1981) ... She is best known as a member of South Korean girl group S.E.S.." 
- Gain — "Son Ga-in (born September 20, 1987) ... She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Brown Eyed Girls." 
- Lina — "Lee Ji-yeon (born February 18, 1984) ... re-debut as a member of South Korean girl group The Grace." 
- Yura — "Kim Ah-young (born November 6, 1992) ... She is best known as a member of the South Korean girl group Girl's Day." 
- Krystal — "Chrystal Soo Jung (born October 24, 1994) ... she debuted in 2009 as a member of South Korean girl group f(x)."

Missing fact / bridge: None of the passages state who "co-starring in Unexpected Love" is. Next retrieval direction: fetch the cast/credits for "Unexpected Love" (search "Unexpected Love cast" or open the Unexpected Love film/TV entry) to match its co-stars against the above candidate names. Use quoted phrases above (name + group) as anchors when matching.
hop2_docs: List of Loving cast and characters | This is a list of actors who appeared on the American soap opera "Loving".
Unexpected Love | Unexpected Love () is a 2014 Chinese romance film directed by Bowie Lau and starring Du Chun.  It was released on 14 February 2014.

Unexpected Love (2017 film) | Unexpected Love (also anown as: "Flying Kite" / "Bi Zui!  Ai Ba" / "Biyeon") is an upcoming Chinese-South Korean romantic comedy film directed by Si-Heup Seong and starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung.
List of Tales of the Unexpected episodes | This is a list of episodes of the British drama series, "Tales of the Unexpected".

The Many Loves of Dobie Gillis (season 1) | This is a list of episodes from the first season of "The Many Loves of Dobie Gillis".  This season of the series explores Dobie Gillis' life as a junior at Central High School, and is the only season of the series to feature Tuesday Weld and Warren Beatty among the cast.

Rektok Ross | Rektok Ross (born May 1, 1980) is an American young-adult fiction writer, journalist, and entertainment host, best known for her contemporary romance novel "Prodigal". "Prodigal" tells the story of a romance between two teenagers from different religious and economic backgrounds that find love in unexpected places.  The novel reached number 1 on The Amazon Best Sellers List for its category in February 2013.

Unexpected (Lumidee album) | Unexpected is the second studio album by American hip-hop and R&B recording artist Lumidee, released by TVT Records on April 17, 2007 in the United States and on June 21, 2007 in the United Kingdom.  The album was produced primarily by J. Marty with contributions made by Scott Storch, T.C. Love, Lenky, Ron Browz, Wyclef Jean, Jerry Duplessis and Red Spyda.
hop2_titles: List of Loving cast and characters
List of Tales of the Unexpected episodes
The Many Loves of Dobie Gillis (season 1)
response.summary_2: Krystal (Chrystal Soo Jung), who debuted as a member of the South Korean girl group f(x), co-stars in Unexpected Love — the film is listed as "starring Zhang Yixing and Krystal Jung."
